title: Summary of Benefits and Coverage
merged_content:
title: Plan Summary
summary: The plan summary covers the period from 01/01/2025 to 12/31/2025 for a family under a PPO plan type. The plan has various deductibles, out-of-pocket limits, and exclusions. The table provides a summary of important questions and answers, including overall deductibles, services covered before deductibles, specific service deductibles, out-of-pocket limits, exclusions from out-of-pocket limits, network provider benefits, and referral requirements for specialists.

title: Medical Event Services
summary: This section outlines the costs associated with common medical events, including visits to healthcare providers, tests, drug treatments, outpatient surgery, and immediate medical attention. The costs vary depending on whether the provider is in-network or out-of-network, with in-network providers being the most cost-effective. The table provides a summary of common medical events and the associated costs.

title: Common Medical Events
summary: This section provides more details on common medical events, including hospital stays, mental health services, pregnancy, recovery, and special health needs. The costs and coverage vary depending on the event and whether the provider is in-network or out-of-network. The table summarizes the common medical events and the associated costs and coverage.

title: Excluded Services
summary: This section lists the services that are generally not covered by the plan, including excluded services and other covered services with limitations. The plan meets the Minimum Value Standards, and language access services are available. The section also provides information on continuing coverage and grievance and appeals rights.

title: Peg's Medical Expenses
summary: This section provides examples of how the plan might cover costs for sample medical situations, including having a baby, managing type 2 diabetes, and treating a simple fracture. The examples include the costs of services, deductibles, copayments, coinsurance, and what is not covered. The total costs for each example are also provided, but it is noted that this is not a cost estimator.

title: Plan Summary
merged_content:
--- PAGE 1 START ---
--- PAGE 1 START ---

PAGE IDENTITY:
Coverage Period: 01/01/2025 - 12/31/2025 Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO The Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) document will help you choose a health plan. The SBC shows you how you and the plan would share the cost for covered health care services. NOTE: Information about the cost of this plan (called the premium) will be provided separately. This is only a summary. For more information about your coverage, or to get a copy of the complete terms of coverage, [insert contact information]. For general definitions of common terms, such as allowed amount, balance billing, coinsurance, copayment, deductible,

NARRATIVE TEXT:
Coverage Period: 01/01/2025 - 12/31/2025 Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO The Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) document will help you choose a health plan. The SBC shows you how you and the plan would share the cost for covered health care services. NOTE: Information about the cost of this plan (called the premium) will be provided separately. This is only a summary. For more information about your coverage, or to get a copy of the complete terms of coverage, [insert contact information]. For general definitions of common terms, such as allowed amount, balance billing, coinsurance, copayment, deductible, provider, or other underlined terms, see the Glossary. You can view the Glossary at [www.insert.com] or call 1-800-[insert] to request a copy.

--- TABLE SUMMARIES ---

**Important Questions and Answers Summary**
==============================================

### Overall Deductible
The overall deductible for this plan is $500 for an individual or $1,000 for a family. Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the plan, each family member must meet their own individual deductible until the total amount of deductible expenses paid by all family members meets the overall family deductible.

### Services Covered Before Deductible
Yes, there are services covered before you meet your deductible. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible. This plan covers some items and services even if you haven't yet met the deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.

### Specific Service Deductibles
There are other deductibles for specific services:
- $300 for prescription drug coverage
- $300 for occupational therapy services
There are no other specific deductibles. You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.

### Out-of-Pocket Limit
The out-of-pocket limit for this plan is:
- $2,500 for an individual / $5,000 for a family for network providers
- $4,000 for an individual / $8,000 for a family for out-of-network providers
The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.

### Exclusions from Out-of-Pocket Limit
The following are not included in the out-of-pocket limit:
- Copayments for certain services
- Premiums
- Balance-billing charges
- Health care that this plan doesn't cover
Even though you pay these expenses, they don't count toward the out-of-pocket limit.

### Network Provider Benefits
Yes, you will pay less if you use a network provider. This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan's network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider's charge and what your plan pays (balance billing). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services. For a list of network providers, see [www.insert.com] or call 1-800-[insert].

### Referral Requirement for Specialists
Yes, you need a referral to see a specialist. This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.

CONTEXT OVERLAP:

--- PAGE 1 END ---

title: Medical Event Services
merged_content:
--- PAGE 2 START ---
--- PAGE 2 START ---

PAGE IDENTITY:
Common Medical Event Services You May Need What You Will Pay Limitations, Exceptions, &Other Important Information Network Provider (You will pay the least) Out-of-Network Provider (You will pay the most) If you visit a health care provider's office or clinic Primary care visit to treat an injury or illness $35 copay/office visit and 20%coinsurance for other outpatient services; deductible does not apply 40% coinsurance None Specialist visit $50 copay/visit 40% coinsurance Preauthorization is required. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service. Preventive care/screening/ immunization No charge 40% coinsurance You

--- TABLE SUMMARIES ---

**Common Medical Event Summary**
=====================================

The following summary outlines the services you may need, what you will pay, and limitations, exceptions, and other important information for various common medical events.

### If you visit a health care provider's office or clinic

* **Primary care visit to treat an injury or illness**
	+ Network Provider: $35 copay/office visit and 20% coinsurance for other outpatient services; deductible does not apply
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: None
* **Specialist visit**
	+ Network Provider: $50 copay/visit
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: Preauthorization is required. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service.
* **Preventive care/screening/immunization**
	+ Network Provider: No charge
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: You may have to pay for services that aren't preventive. Ask your provider if the services needed are preventive. Then check what your plan will pay for.

### If you have a test

* **Diagnostic test (x-ray, blood work)**
	+ Network Provider: $10 copay/test
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: None
* **Imaging (CT/PET scans, MRIs)**
	+ Network Provider: $50 copay/test
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: None

### If you need drugs to treat your illness or condition

* **Generic drugs (Tier 1)**
	+ Network Provider: $10 copay/prescription (retail & mail order)
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: Covers up to a 30-day supply (retail subscription); 31-90 day supply (mail order prescription).
* **Preferred brand drugs (Tier 2)**
	+ Network Provider: $30 copay/prescription (retail & mail order)
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: Covers up to a 30-day supply (retail subscription); 31-90 day supply (mail order prescription).
* **Non-preferred brand drugs (Tier 3)**
	+ Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 60% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: Covers up to a 30-day supply (retail subscription); 31-90 day supply (mail order prescription).
* **Specialty drugs (Tier 4)**
	+ Network Provider: 50% coinsurance
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 70% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: Covers up to a 30-day supply (retail subscription); 31-90 day supply (mail order prescription).

### If you have outpatient surgery

* **Facility fee (e.g., ambulatory surgery center)**
	+ Network Provider: $100/day copay
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: Preauthorization is required. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service. 50% coinsurance for anesthesia.
* **Physician/surgeon fees**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: Preauthorization is required. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service. 50% coinsurance for anesthesia.

### If you need immediate medical attention

* **Emergency room care**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 20% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: None
* **Emergency medical transportation**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 20% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: None
* **Urgent care**
	+ Network Provider: $30 copay/visit
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance
	+ Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information: None

CONTEXT OVERLAP:
Common Medical Event Services You May Need What You Will Pay Limitations, Exceptions, &Other Important Information Network Provider (You will pay the least) Out-of-Network Provider (You will pay the most) I

--- PAGE 2 END ---

title: Common Medical Events
merged_content:
--- PAGE 3 START ---
--- PAGE 3 START ---

PAGE IDENTITY:
Common Medical Event Services You May Need What You Will Pay Limitations, Exceptions, &Other Important Information Network Provider (You will pay the least) Out-of-Network Provider (You will pay the most) If you have a hospital stay Facility fee (e.g., hospital room) 20% coinsurance 40% coinsurance Preauthorization is required. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service. Physician/surgeon fees 20% coinsurance 40% coinsurance 50% coinsurance for anesthesia. If you need mental health, behavioral health, or substance abuse services Outpatient services $35 copay/office visit and 20%coinsurance for other outpatient services 40% coinsurance

--- TABLE SUMMARIES ---

**Common Medical Event Summary**
=====================================

The following summary outlines the services you may need, what you will pay, and limitations, exceptions, and other important information for various common medical events.

### If you have a hospital stay

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Facility fee (e.g., hospital room)
	+ Physician/surgeon fees
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance for facility fee and physician/surgeon fees
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for facility fee and physician/surgeon fees
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ Preauthorization is required. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service.
	+ 50% coinsurance for anesthesia

### If you need mental health, behavioral health, or substance abuse services

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Outpatient services
	+ Inpatient services
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: $35 copay/office visit and 20% coinsurance for other outpatient services, 20% coinsurance for inpatient services
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for outpatient and inpatient services
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ None

### If you are pregnant

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Office visits
	+ Childbirth/delivery professional services
	+ Childbirth/delivery facility services
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance for office visits, childbirth/delivery professional services, and childbirth/delivery facility services
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for office visits, childbirth/delivery professional services, and childbirth/delivery facility services
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ Cost sharing does not apply for preventive services. Depending on the type of services, a coinsurance may apply. Maternity care may include tests and services described elsewhere in the SBC (i.e., ultrasound).

### If you need help recovering or have other special health needs

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Home health care
	+ Rehabilitation services
	+ Habilitation services
	+ Skilled nursing care
	+ Durable medical equipment
	+ Hospice services
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance for all services
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for all services
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ 60 visits/year for home health care and rehabilitation services
	+ Includes physical therapy, speech therapy, and occupational therapy for rehabilitation services
	+ Excludes vehicle modifications, home modifications, exercise, and bathroom equipment for durable medical equipment
	+ Preauthorization is required for hospice services. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service.

### If your child needs dental or eye care

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Children's eye exam
	+ Children's glasses
	+ Children's dental check-up
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: $35 copay/visit for children's eye exam, 20% coinsurance for children's glasses, no charge for children's dental check-up
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: Not covered for children's eye exam and glasses, not covered for children's dental check-up
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ Coverage limited to one exam/year for children's eye exam
	+ Coverage limited to one pair of glasses/year for children's glasses
	+ None for children's dental check-up

--- PAGE 3 END ---

=====================================

### If you have a hospital stay

* **Services You May Need:**

	+ Physician/surgeon fees
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance for facility fee and physician/surgeon fees
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for facility fee and physician/surgeon fees
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ Preauthorization is required. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service.
	+ 50% coinsurance for anesthesia

### If you need mental health, behavioral health, or substance abuse services

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Outpatient services
	+ Inpatient services
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: $35 copay/office visit and 20% coinsurance for other outpatient services, 20% coinsurance for inpatient services
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for outpatient and inpatient services
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ None

### If you are pregnant

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Office visits
	+ Childbirth/delivery professional services
	+ Childbirth/delivery facility services
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance for office visits, childbirth/delivery professional services, and childbirth/delivery facility services
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for office visits, childbirth/delivery professional services, and childbirth/delivery facility services
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ Cost sharing does not apply for preventive services. Depending on the type of services, a coinsurance may apply. Maternity care may include tests and services described elsewhere in the SBC (i.e., ultrasound).

### If you need help recovering or have other special health needs

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Home health care
	+ Rehabilitation services
	+ Habilitation services
	+ Skilled nursing care
	+ Durable medical equipment
	+ Hospice services
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: 20% coinsurance for all services
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: 40% coinsurance for all services
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ 60 visits/year for home health care and rehabilitation services
	+ Includes physical therapy, speech therapy, and occupational therapy for rehabilitation services
	+ Excludes vehicle modifications, home modifications, exercise, and bathroom equipment for durable medical equipment
	+ Preauthorization is required for hospice services. If you don't get preauthorization, benefits could be reduced by 50% of the total cost of the service.

### If your child needs dental or eye care

* **Services You May Need:**
	+ Children's eye exam
	+ Children's glasses
	+ Children's dental check-up
* **What You Will Pay:**
	+ Network Provider: $35 copay/visit for children's eye exam, 20% coinsurance for children's glasses, no charge for children's dental check-up
	+ Out-of-Network Provider: Not covered for children's eye exam and glasses, not covered for children's dental check-up
* **Limitations, Exceptions, & Other Important Information:**
	+ Coverage limited to one exam/year for children's eye exam
	+ Coverage limited to one pair of glasses/year for children's glasses
	+ None for children's dental check-up]

--- PAGE 3 END ---

Excluded Services & Other Covered Services: Services Your Plan Generally Does NOT Cover (Check your policy or plan document for more information and a list of any other excluded services.) Cosmetic surgery Dental care (Adult) Infertility treatment Long-term care Non-emergency care when traveling outside the U.S. Private-duty nursing Other Covered Services (Limitations may apply to these services. This isn't a complete list. Please see your plan document.) Acupuncture (if prescribed for rehabilitation purposes) Bariatric surgery Your Rights to Continue Coverage: There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other coverage options may be available to you, too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.Health Care.gov or call 1-800-3182596. Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information on how to submit a claim, appeal, or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions]. Does this plan provide Minimum Essential Coverage? Yes. Minimum Essential Coverage generally includes plans, health insurance available through the Marketplace or other individual market policies, Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP, TRICARE, and certain other coverage. If you are eligible for certain types of Minimum Essential Coverage, you may not be eligible for the premium tax credit. Does this plan meet the Minimum Value Standards? Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace. Language Access Services: Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number]. Chamorro (Chamoru): Para un ma ayuda gi finu Chamoru, å'gang [insert telephone number]. To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section. PRA Disclosure Statement: According to the Paperwork Reduction Act of 1995, no persons are required to respond to a collection of information unless it displays a valid OMB control number. The valid OMB control number for this information collection is 0938-1146 . The time required to complete this information collection is estimated to average 0.02 hours per response, including the time to review instructions, search existing data resources, gather the data needed, and complete and review the information collection. If you have comments concerning the accuracy of the time estimate(s) or suggestions for improving this form, please write to: CMS, 7500 Security Boulevard, Attn: PRA Reports Clearance Officer, Mail Stop C 4-26-05, Baltimore, Maryland 21244-1850. Peg is Having a Baby (9 months of in-network pre-natal care and a hospital

NARRATIVE TEXT:
PAGE_LEVEL_SUMMARY:
About these Coverage Examples
Summary: The provided coverage examples are not cost estimators, but rather illustrations of how a health plan might cover medical care, with actual costs varying depending on the care received, provider prices, and other factors. The examples are based on self-only coverage and focus on cost-sharing amounts, deductibles, copayments, coinsurance, and excluded services. These examples can be used to compare the portion of costs that might be paid under different health plans.

Peg is Having a Baby
Summary: This example involves 9 months of in-network pre-natal care and a hospital delivery, with the plan's overall deductible being $500, specialist copayment being $50, hospital coinsurance being 20%, and other coinsurance being 20%. The example includes services such as specialist office visits, childbirth/delivery professional services, diagnostic tests, and specialist visits. The total example cost is not explicitly stated, but Peg would pay $12,700, with a breakdown of $500 in deductibles, $200 in copayments, $1,800 in coinsurance, $60 in other costs, and $2,560 in additional coinsurance, totaling $2,560 in coinsurance and other costs.

Managing Joe's Type 2 Diabetes
Summary: This example involves a year of routine in-network care for a well-controlled condition, with the plan's overall deductible being $500, specialist copayment being $50, hospital coinsurance being 20%, and other coinsurance being 20%. The example includes services such as primary care physician office visits, diagnostic tests, prescription drugs, and durable medical equipment. The total example cost is $5,600, and Joe would pay $1,820, with a breakdown of $800 in deductibles, $900 in copayments, $100 in coinsurance, and $20 in other costs.

Mia's Simple Fracture
Summary: This example involves an in-network emergency room visit and follow-up care, with the plan's overall deductible being $500, specialist copayment being $50, hospital coinsurance being 20%, and other coinsurance being 20%. The example includes services such as emergency room care, diagnostic tests, durable medical equipment, and rehabilitation services. The total example cost is $2,800, and Mia would pay $1,100, with a breakdown of $500 in deductibles, $200 in copayments, $400 in coinsurance, and $0 in other costs.

Note on Wellness Program
Summary: The provided numbers assume the patient does not participate in the plan's wellness program, which may offer cost reductions. For more information about the wellness program, patients can contact the plan. Additionally, the plan has other deductibles for specific services included in the coverage examples, which can be found in the "Are there other deductibles for specific services?" section.
